user_query: How can we go beyond correlations to establish causal claims about health determinants?
ai_response_summary: Outlined a multi-pronged causal inference toolkit: (1) DAG specification using DoWhy with backdoor criterion to identify confounders, (2) Instrumental Variable estimation (2SLS) using geographic/climatic instruments for health expenditure effects, (3) Difference-in-Differences for policy evaluations like UHC adoption, (4) Mediation analysis (Baron-Kenny) to decompose GDP→LE into direct and health-expenditure-mediated pathways, (5) E-values for unmeasured confounding sensitivity. Emphasized that most competitors will only run correlations, making this a key differentiator.
outcome: Implemented causal.py module with build_causal_dag(), iv_2sls(), difference_in_differences(), mediation_analysis(), compute_e_value()
